retrospective.harder_than_needed: AC-ETE-2 as literally worded ("the existing named-user path... must continue to pass unmodified") turned out to be false of the actual, already-shipped app: S4's auto-accept (stories.json marks S1-S4 'done') writes an outbound join-request record on EVERY successful sendJoinRequest with no gate on whether the requester has a name, so the pre-existing named-user test's final assertion (poll for a pending-invitation row, then click Accept) now times out against the real app -- the named path auto-accepts too, exactly like the new nameless path. I verified this empirically (ran the untouched test against the live app before changing anything, watched it fail exactly as predicted, then fixed the assertion to match shipped behavior) rather than trusting the AC's literal wording, which cost a full docker relay cycle to confirm. I also lost real time to a self-inflicted e2e-runner artifact: backgrounding `make test-e2e-all` through a `| tail -N` pipe produces zero streamed output until the whole pipeline's EOF (tail buffers), so a killed/long-running background run looked indistinguishable from 'silently still working' -- the fix was to stop piping through tail and run the scoped spec directly in the foreground instead, which the coordinator's message also independently pointed me toward.
retrospective.surprised_by: The full `make test-e2e-all` gate could not be completed in this session (see below) -- not from a defect in this story's changes, but from a tooling/environment friction chain: (1) an early, un-wiped-relay dev-iteration run left stale gift-wrap events that a later same-file test run re-processed, producing one spurious failure that a clean relay wipe fully resolved; (2) the subsequent attempt to run the definitive full-suite gate via `timeout 590 make test-e2e-all 2>&1 | tail -250` under a background bash call produced no usable output at all (tail's buffering + the harness's own 120s auto-backgrounding conversion interacted badly), and by the time this was noticed the underlying process had already exited with nothing captured.
